title: Workflow using openalexPro2
text: package openalexPro2 extension package openalexR. main difference openalexR processing memory returns result R object, openalexPro2 write results disk. allows processing large corpora. processing done using duckdb package makes possible tro complex queries corpora without need load whole corpus memory.

title: Workflow using openalexPro2
text: workflow using openalexPro2 essentially follows: function openalexPro2::pro_query() used build API query OpenAlex API function openalexPro2::pro_request() used retrieve results OpenAlex API store folder format oif json files returned OpenAlex. function openalexPro2::pro_request_jsonl() cleaning editing json files Json Lines, aka NDJSON, atext file line separate JSON object. details see function openalexPro2::pro_request_jsonl_parquet() used convert json files parquet database TODO: Add plantuml graph TODO add info examles

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: pro_query() function foundation openalexPro workflow. constructs well-formed URLs querying OpenAlex API, handling parameter validation, filter construction, automatic request chunking large queries. vignette provides comprehensive guide using pro_query(), including: Basic usage patterns Advanced filtering selection Automatic chunking large queries Error handling validation Internal architecture flow

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: basic query requires entity type: Add search term perform full-text search: Use select parameter specify fields return: Note: function validates field names opt_select_fields() provides helpful suggestions typos:
code: library(openalexPro)  # Query for works url <- pro_query(entity = "works") url url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate change biodiversity" ) url url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "machine learning",   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year", "cited_by_count") ) url # This will error with a suggestion try(   pro_query(     entity = "works",     select = c("id", "title") # Should be "ids" not "id"   ) ) # Error: Invalid select field(s): id. # Did you mean: id → ids? # Valid select field(s) are defined in `opt_select_fields()`.

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: basic query requires entity type:
code: library(openalexPro)  # Query for works url <- pro_query(entity = "works") url

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Add search term perform full-text search:
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate change biodiversity" ) url

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Use select parameter specify fields return: Note: function validates field names opt_select_fields() provides helpful suggestions typos:
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "machine learning",   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year", "cited_by_count") ) url # This will error with a suggestion try(   pro_query(     entity = "works",     select = c("id", "title") # Should be "ids" not "id"   ) ) # Error: Invalid select field(s): id. # Did you mean: id → ids? # Valid select field(s) are defined in `opt_select_fields()`.

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Filters passed named arguments via ...: Pass vector express logic within filter: Filter names validated opt_filter_names():
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   from_publication_date = "2020-01-01",   to_publication_date = "2023-12-31",   type = "article" ) url url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   language = c("en", "de", "fr"), # English OR German OR French   type = c("article", "preprint") # Article OR Preprint ) url # This will error with suggestions try(   pro_query(     entity = "works",     publiction_year = 2023 # Typo: should be "publication_year"   ) ) # Error: Invalid filter name(s): publiction_year. # Did you mean: publiction_year → publication_year? # Valid filter names are defined in `opt_filter_names()`.

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Filters passed named arguments via ...:
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   from_publication_date = "2020-01-01",   to_publication_date = "2023-12-31",   type = "article" ) url

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Pass vector express logic within filter:
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   language = c("en", "de", "fr"), # English OR German OR French   type = c("article", "preprint") # Article OR Preprint ) url

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Filter names validated opt_filter_names():
code: # This will error with suggestions try(   pro_query(     entity = "works",     publiction_year = 2023 # Typo: should be "publication_year"   ) ) # Error: Invalid filter name(s): publiction_year. # Did you mean: publiction_year → publication_year? # Valid filter names are defined in `opt_filter_names()`.

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Use group_by get aggregate counts: options parameter accepts additional query parameters: Fetch specific entity ID:
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "artificial intelligence",   group_by = "publication_year" ) url url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "quantum computing",   options = list(     per_page = 200,     sort = "cited_by_count:desc",     cursor = "*"   ) ) url url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   id = "W2741809807" ) url

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Use group_by get aggregate counts:
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "artificial intelligence",   group_by = "publication_year" ) url

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: options parameter accepts additional query parameters:
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "quantum computing",   options = list(     per_page = 200,     sort = "cited_by_count:desc",     cursor = "*"   ) ) url

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Fetch specific entity ID:
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   id = "W2741809807" ) url

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: querying large lists DOIs IDs, pro_query() automatically splits request chunks avoid API limits. default, requests chunked 50 items. following filters trigger automatic chunking: openalex ids.openalex doi cites cited_by Adjust chunk size chunk_limit:
code: # Create a list of 120 DOIs dois <- paste0("10.1234/example", 1:120)  # This returns a named list of URLs urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = dois,   chunk_limit = 50 # Default )  # Check the result length(urls) # 3 chunks (50 + 50 + 20) names(urls) # "chunk_1", "chunk_2", "chunk_3" urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = dois,   chunk_limit = 100 # Larger chunks )  length(urls) # 2 chunks (100 + 20)

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: default, requests chunked 50 items. following filters trigger automatic chunking: openalex ids.openalex doi cites cited_by

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
code: # Create a list of 120 DOIs dois <- paste0("10.1234/example", 1:120)  # This returns a named list of URLs urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = dois,   chunk_limit = 50 # Default )  # Check the result length(urls) # 3 chunks (50 + 50 + 20) names(urls) # "chunk_1", "chunk_2", "chunk_3"

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Adjust chunk size chunk_limit:
code: urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = dois,   chunk_limit = 100 # Larger chunks )  length(urls) # 2 chunks (100 + 20)

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: pro_query() supports major OpenAlex entities:
code: # Works pro_query(entity = "works", search = "neuroscience")  # Authors pro_query(entity = "authors", search = "Einstein")  # Institutions pro_query(entity = "institutions", search = "MIT")  # Venues (journals, conferences) pro_query(entity = "venues", search = "Nature")  # Concepts (topics/fields) pro_query(entity = "concepts", search = "machine learning")  # Publishers pro_query(entity = "publishers", search = "Springer")  # Funders pro_query(entity = "funders", search = "NSF")

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: function uses several internal helpers clean, maintainable code: Checks object NULL zero length. Collapses vectors pipe-separated strings API. Constructs filter query string named list. Provides spelling suggestions invalid field names using edit distance. Constructs helpful error messages suggestions. Validate field names filter names allowed values. chunking algorithm designed handle multiple chunkable filters simultaneously:
code:
flowchart TD Start([pro_query called]) --> ValidateEntity[Validate entity type] ValidateEntity --> GatherFilters[Gather filters from ...] GatherFilters --> CheckMultipleID{Multiple IDs
provided?} CheckMultipleID -->|Yes| MoveToFilter[Move IDs to
ids.openalex filter] CheckMultipleID -->|No| ValidateInputs[Validate filters
and select fields] MoveToFilter --> ValidateInputs ValidateInputs --> CheckChunking{Filters contain
chunkable fields?} CheckChunking -->|No| SingleBatch[Single batch:
filter_batches = list] CheckChunking -->|Yes| ChunkLoop[Split large filters
into chunks] ChunkLoop --> CheckSize{Values > chunk_limit?} CheckSize -->|Yes| SplitValues[Split values into
groups of chunk_limit] CheckSize -->|No| KeepIntact[Keep filter intact] SplitValues --> NextFilter{More chunkable
filters?} KeepIntact --> NextFilter NextFilter -->|Yes| ChunkLoop NextFilter -->|No| BuildURLs SingleBatch --> BuildURLs[Build URLs for each batch] BuildURLs --> ForEachBatch[For each batch:
1. Build filter string
2. Build select string
3. Assemble query params
4. Create URL] ForEachBatch --> CheckCount{Multiple batches?} CheckCount -->|Yes| ReturnList[Return named list:
chunk_1, chunk_2, ...] CheckCount -->|No| ReturnSingle[Return single URL] ReturnList --> End([Return URLs]) ReturnSingle --> End style Start fill:#e1f5e1 style End fill:#e1f5e1 style ValidateInputs fill:#fff3cd style ChunkLoop fill:#cce5ff style BuildURLs fill:#f8d7da # Used throughout for consistent NULL/empty checking .is_empty(NULL) # TRUE .is_empty(character()) # TRUE .is_empty("value") # FALSE # Handles NULL, NA values, and logical conversion .oa_collapse(c("en", "de", "fr")) # "en|de|fr" .oa_collapse(c(TRUE, FALSE)) # "true|false" .oa_collapse(c("a", NA, "b")) # "a|b" # Combines multiple filters with commas filters <- list( from_publication_date = "2020-01-01", language = c("en", "de"), type = "article" ) .oa_build_filter(filters) # "from_publication_date:2020-01-01,language:en|de,type:article" # Suggests corrections within edit distance of 3 .fuzzy_suggest("titel", c("title", "author", "year")) # "title" .fuzzy_suggest("publiction_year", opt_filter_names()) # "publication_year" flowchart TD Start([Start with filter_batches
containing original filter]) --> IdentifyTargets[Identify chunk targets
in current filters] IdentifyTargets --> LoopTargets{For each
chunk target} LoopTargets --> LoopBatches{For each
current batch} LoopBatches --> GetValues[Get values for
this target key] GetValues --> RemoveNA[Remove NA values] RemoveNA --> CheckSize{length > chunk_limit?} CheckSize -->|Yes| Split[Split into chunks
of chunk_limit] CheckSize -->|No| Keep[Keep batch as-is] Split --> CreateBatches[Create new batches
with chunked values] Keep --> AddToNew[Add to new_batches] CreateBatches --> AddToNew AddToNew --> MoreBatches{More batches
to process?} MoreBatches -->|Yes| LoopBatches MoreBatches -->|No| UpdateBatches[filter_batches = new_batches] UpdateBatches --> MoreTargets{More targets
to process?} MoreTargets -->|Yes| LoopTargets MoreTargets -->|No| Return([Return filter_batches]) style Start fill:#e1f5e1 style Return fill:#e1f5e1 style CheckSize fill:#fff3cd style Split fill:#f8d7da # Both DOI and cites will be chunked urls <- pro_query( entity = "works", doi = paste0("10.1234/example", 1:75), # 75 DOIs cites = paste0("W", 1000:1089) # 90 IDs ) # Result: 75/50 = 2 DOI chunks, 90/50 = 2 cites chunks # Cross product: 2 × 2 = 4 total URL batches length(urls) # 4

code:
flowchart TD Start([pro_query called]) --> ValidateEntity[Validate entity type] ValidateEntity --> GatherFilters[Gather filters from ...] GatherFilters --> CheckMultipleID{Multiple IDs
provided?} CheckMultipleID -->|Yes| MoveToFilter[Move IDs to
ids.openalex filter] CheckMultipleID -->|No| ValidateInputs[Validate filters
and select fields] MoveToFilter --> ValidateInputs ValidateInputs --> CheckChunking{Filters contain
chunkable fields?} CheckChunking -->|No| SingleBatch[Single batch:
filter_batches = list] CheckChunking -->|Yes| ChunkLoop[Split large filters
into chunks] ChunkLoop --> CheckSize{Values > chunk_limit?} CheckSize -->|Yes| SplitValues[Split values into
groups of chunk_limit] CheckSize -->|No| KeepIntact[Keep filter intact] SplitValues --> NextFilter{More chunkable
filters?} KeepIntact --> NextFilter NextFilter -->|Yes| ChunkLoop NextFilter -->|No| BuildURLs SingleBatch --> BuildURLs[Build URLs for each batch] BuildURLs --> ForEachBatch[For each batch:
1. Build filter string
2. Build select string
3. Assemble query params
4. Create URL] ForEachBatch --> CheckCount{Multiple batches?} CheckCount -->|Yes| ReturnList[Return named list:
chunk_1, chunk_2, ...] CheckCount -->|No| ReturnSingle[Return single URL] ReturnList --> End([Return URLs]) ReturnSingle --> End style Start fill:#e1f5e1 style End fill:#e1f5e1 style ValidateInputs fill:#fff3cd style ChunkLoop fill:#cce5ff style BuildURLs fill:#f8d7da

text: chunking algorithm designed handle multiple chunkable filters simultaneously:
code:
flowchart TD Start([Start with filter_batches
containing original filter]) --> IdentifyTargets[Identify chunk targets
in current filters] IdentifyTargets --> LoopTargets{For each
chunk target} LoopTargets --> LoopBatches{For each
current batch} LoopBatches --> GetValues[Get values for
this target key] GetValues --> RemoveNA[Remove NA values] RemoveNA --> CheckSize{length > chunk_limit?} CheckSize -->|Yes| Split[Split into chunks
of chunk_limit] CheckSize -->|No| Keep[Keep batch as-is] Split --> CreateBatches[Create new batches
with chunked values] Keep --> AddToNew[Add to new_batches] CreateBatches --> AddToNew AddToNew --> MoreBatches{More batches
to process?} MoreBatches -->|Yes| LoopBatches MoreBatches -->|No| UpdateBatches[filter_batches = new_batches] UpdateBatches --> MoreTargets{More targets
to process?} MoreTargets -->|Yes| LoopTargets MoreTargets -->|No| Return([Return filter_batches]) style Start fill:#e1f5e1 style Return fill:#e1f5e1 style CheckSize fill:#fff3cd style Split fill:#f8d7da # Both DOI and cites will be chunked urls <- pro_query( entity = "works", doi = paste0("10.1234/example", 1:75), # 75 DOIs cites = paste0("W", 1000:1089) # 90 IDs ) # Result: 75/50 = 2 DOI chunks, 90/50 = 2 cites chunks # Cross product: 2 × 2 = 4 total URL batches length(urls) # 4

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
code: # Both DOI and cites will be chunked urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = paste0("10.1234/example", 1:75), # 75 DOIs   cites = paste0("W", 1000:1089) # 90 IDs )  # Result: 75/50 = 2 DOI chunks, 90/50 = 2 cites chunks # Cross product: 2 × 2 = 4 total URL batches length(urls) # 4

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Always use select limit returned fields reduce API response size: Let pro_query() handle large ID lists automatically: Use validation helpers check filters making requests: large result sets, use appropriate options:
code: # Good: Only request needed fields pro_query(   entity = "works",   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year") )  # Avoid: Requesting all fields (default behavior without select) # The function handles chunking transparently urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = large_doi_vector # Even thousands of DOIs )  # Then iterate over chunks in your request logic for (url in urls) {   # Process each chunk... } # Check available filters opt_filter_names()  # Check available select fields opt_select_fields() pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate change",   options = list(     per_page = 200, # Maximum page size     sort = "publication_date:desc",     cursor = "*" # For cursor pagination   ) )

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Always use select limit returned fields reduce API response size:
code: # Good: Only request needed fields pro_query(   entity = "works",   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year") )  # Avoid: Requesting all fields (default behavior without select)

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: Let pro_query() handle large ID lists automatically:
code: # The function handles chunking transparently urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = large_doi_vector # Even thousands of DOIs )  # Then iterate over chunks in your request logic for (url in urls) {   # Process each chunk... }

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   from_publication_date = "2024-01-01",   type = "article",   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_date", "authorships"),   options = list(     per_page = 200,     sort = "publication_date:desc"   ) ) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   from_cited_by_count = 100,   type = "article",   select = c("ids", "title", "cited_by_count", "publication_year"),   options = list(     sort = "cited_by_count:desc",     per_page = 50   ) ) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   `author.id` = "A2208157607", # Use backticks for dots in names   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year"),   options = list(sort = "publication_date:desc") ) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   `institutions.id` = "I4200000001",   from_publication_date = "2020-01-01",   type = "article",   select = c("ids", "title", "authorships", "publication_year") ) # Read DOIs from file dois <- readLines("my_dois.txt")  # Query in chunks automatically urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = dois,   select = c("ids", "title", "cited_by_count") )  # Process each chunk results <- lapply(urls, function(url) {   # Use pro_request() or similar to fetch data   # pro_request(url) })

code: try(pro_query(entity = "paper")) # Error: 'arg' should be one of "works", "authors", "venues", #        "institutions", "concepts", "publishers", "funders" try(pro_query(entity = "works", invalid_filter = "value")) # Error: Invalid filter name(s): invalid_filter. # Valid filter names are defined in `opt_filter_names()`. try(pro_query(entity = "works", select = c("id", "titel"))) # Error: Invalid select field(s): id, titel. # Did you mean: id → ids, titel → title? # Valid select fields are defined in `opt_select_fields()`.

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: pro_query() first step typical openalexPro workflow:
code: library(openalexPro)  # Step 1: Build query urls <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "machine learning healthcare",   from_publication_date = "2020-01-01",   type = "article",   select = c("ids", "title", "abstract", "publication_year") )  # Step 2: Retrieve data # pro_request(urls, output_dir = "data/json")  # Step 3: Convert to JSONL # pro_request_jsonl("data/json", "data/jsonl")  # Step 4: Convert to Parquet # pro_request_jsonl_parquet("data/jsonl", "data/parquet")  # Step 5: Query with DuckDB # ... analyze your data ...

title: Building OpenAlex API Queries with pro_query()
text: opt_filter_names() - List available filter names opt_select_fields() - List available select fields pro_request() - Execute API requests save results pro_request_jsonl() - Convert JSON JSONL format pro_request_jsonl_parquet() - Convert JSONL Parquet format

title: Providing a more advanced access to OpenAlex for the power user
text: package builds package openalexR provides advanced approach retrieve works OpenAlex. contrast openalexR, processing conversions memory. processing memory advantages smaller smaller number records retrieved OpenAlex, limits te number works can retrieved due memory limitations. Even limit reached, often occurring new allocation memory slows processing. first step, contrast, openalexPro uses disc processing approach data processed number records returned per call, .e. per page processing approach.

title: Providing a more advanced access to OpenAlex for the power user
text: retrieval works initial processing / preparation can split three steps: first step (openalexPro::pro_request()), page API call saved individual json file returned API. number retrieved records effectively limited space drive json files saved. complete responses including metadata saved, one end use custom made code process responses, .e. ingest database. second step (openalexPro::pro_request_jsonl()), json files processed per file basis using jq command-line json processor. step abstract text re-constructed, citation string work generated, optionally add page field added. writes resulting json file newline-delimited JSON (.jsonl), suitable processing using arrow DuckDB. Int third (final) step (openalexPro::pro_request_jsonl_parquet()) converts jsonl files parquet database partitioned page using duckdb package. , processing done per page well, conversion limited memory. approach results stable pipeline works retrieval small well large huge corpora. processing done per page (maximum 200 works), scaling less linear (one application, 4 million works retrieved without problems). One point needs taken consideration retrieving huge corpora, rate limits OpenAlex (see details). final format used package save retrieved data parquet format space efficient allows disc processing, therefor need load complete data memory (see detailed description format well r-package arrow). use disc processing R, arrow packages interfaces directly dplyr, one can lot processing retrieving actual data memory (see section dplyr arrow well general arrow chapter Hadleys Wickhams R Data Science (2e) bookhttps://r4ds.hadley.nz).

title: Providing a more advanced access to OpenAlex for the power user
text: latest “stable” version available via r-universe “development” version can installed github. generally recommended! Unless need bleeding edge functionality can deal changing function definitions, whant test new functionality, recommended.
code: install.packages('openalexPro', repos = c('https://rkrug.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org')) remotes::install_github("rkrug/openalexPro", ref = "dev")

title: Render and open the compatibility report — compatibility_report
text: output_dir Directory write rendered HTML data . Defaults flder `./Compatibility Report`. open Logical; `TRUE` (default) opens rendered HTML system browser. quiet Logical; suppress rendering output `TRUE`. Default: `FALSE`.

title: Render and open the compatibility report — compatibility_report
text: Invisibly returns path rendered HTML file.

title: Render and open the compatibility report — compatibility_report
text: report designed help validate client–API compatibility real time. rendering, report performs live requests OpenAlex API compares responses package's expected behavior. cached data used: every section issues fresh API calls output reflects current state upstream service. report summarizes differences fields, types, pagination response shapes surface potential regressions upstream changes local client updates. Note: depends live API calls, rendering may take longer requires network access. mindful API rate limits running report repeatedly.

what: Extract DOIs or Components from Character Vectors — extract_doi
title: Extract DOIs or Components from Character Vectors — extract_doi
text: Extracts DOIs specific DOI components (resolver, prefix, suffix) character vector. Assumes element `x` contains one DOI (without resolver).

title: Extract DOIs or Components from Character Vectors — extract_doi
code: extract_doi(   x,   non_doi_value = "",   normalize = TRUE,   what = c("doi", "resolver", "prefix", "suffix") )

title: Extract DOIs or Components from Character Vectors — extract_doi
text: x character vector potentially containing DOIs (e.g., raw DOIs, DOI URLs, strings embedded DOIs). non_doi_value Value use elements DOI component found. `NULL`, matched elements returned. normalize Logical. `TRUE` (default), convert extracted DOIs suffixes lowercase trim surrounding whitespace. effect `= "prefix"` `= "resolver"`. extract element. One : "doi" full DOI name (prefix + "/" + suffix). Example: `"10.5281/zenodo.1234567"` (default) "resolver" resolver URL (e.g., `"https://doi.org/"`, `"http://dx.doi.org/"`) present "prefix" DOI prefix (e.g., `"10.5281"`) "suffix" DOI suffix (e.g., `"zenodo.1234567"`)

title: Extract DOIs or Components from Character Vectors — extract_doi
text: character vector:   - `non_doi_value` `NULL`, vector length `x`, unmatched entries replaced.   - `non_doi_value` `NULL`, vector matched entries.

title: Extract DOIs or Components from Character Vectors — extract_doi
code: x <- c(   "https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.1234567",   " 10.1000/XYZ456  ",   "no doi here",   NA )  extract_doi(x)  # Full DOIs (default) #> [1] "10.5281/zenodo.1234567" "10.1000/xyz456"         ""                       #> [4] ""                       extract_doi(x, what = "resolver") #> [1] "https://doi.org/" ""                 ""                 ""                 extract_doi(x, what = "prefix") #> [1] "10.5281" "10.1000" ""        ""        extract_doi(x, what = "suffix") #> [1] "zenodo.1234567" "xyz456"         ""               ""               extract_doi(x, non_doi_value = NA_character_) #> [1] "10.5281/zenodo.1234567" "10.1000/xyz456"         NA                       #> [4] NA                       extract_doi(x, non_doi_value = NULL) #> [1] "10.5281/zenodo.1234567" "10.1000/xyz456"

what: Execute a jq transformation from an OpenAlex-style JSON to JSONL — jq_execute
title: Execute a jq transformation from an OpenAlex-style JSON to JSONL — jq_execute
text: function runs jq filter extract records "results" array (root type = "single"), reconstruct abstract text, generate citation string, optionally add page field. writes result newline-delimited JSON (.jsonl), suitable Arrow DuckDB. details jq filter logic, see vignette("jq", package = "openalexPro").

title: Execute a jq transformation from an OpenAlex-style JSON to JSONL — jq_execute
code: jq_execute(   input_json,   output_jsonl,   add_columns = list(),   jq_filter = NULL,   page = NULL,   type = c("results", "single", "group_by") )

title: Execute a jq transformation from an OpenAlex-style JSON to JSONL — jq_execute
text: input_json Path input JSON file output_jsonl Path output .jsonl file add_columns List additional fields added output. nave provided named list, e./g. `list(column_1 = "value_1", column_2 = 2)`. Scalar values supported. jq_filter Optional custom jq filter string. NULL, default filter used. page Optional integer added "page" field output record type Either "results" (default, expects .results[] array) "single" (treat input array records directly)

title: Execute a jq transformation from an OpenAlex-style JSON to JSONL — jq_execute
text: Invisibly returns output path

TRUE, output deleted already exists. mailto email address user. api_key API key user. workers Number parallel workers use query_url list. Defaults 1. verbose Logical indicating whether show verbose messages. progress Logical indicating whether show progress bar. Default TRUE. count_only use . function abort set TRUE give warning FALSE error_log location error log API calls. (default: NULL (none)).

title: Build an OpenAlex request (httr2) — pro_query
text: Construct httr2 request OpenAlex API. filters must supplied named ... arguments (e.g., from_publication_date = "2020-01-01").

title: Build an OpenAlex request (httr2) — pro_query
code: pro_query(   entity = c("works", "authors", "venues", "institutions", "concepts", "publishers",     "funders"),   id = NULL,   search = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   select = NULL,   options = NULL,   endpoint = "https://api.openalex.org",   chunk_limit = 50L,   ... )

title: Build an OpenAlex request (httr2) — pro_query
text: entity Character; one "works", "authors", "venues", "institutions", "concepts", "publishers", "funders". id Optional ID vector IDs (e.g., "W1775749144"). single ID provided, fetches one entity directly. multiple IDs provided, automatically moved ids.openalex filter. search Optional full-text search string. group_by Optional field group (facets), e.g. "type". select Optional character vector fields return. options Optional named list additional query parameters (e.g., list(per_page = 200, sort = "cited_by_count:desc", cursor = "*", sample = 100)). endpoint Base API URL. Defaults "https://api.openalex.org". chunk_limit Number DOIS ids per chunk chunked. Default: 50 ... Filters named arguments. Values may scalars vectors (vectors collapsed "|" express ).

title: Build an OpenAlex request (httr2) — pro_query
text: individual URL list URLs.

title: Build an OpenAlex request (httr2) — pro_query
text: Filter names validated via .validate_filter() using opt_filter_names(). select fields validated via .validate_select() using `opt_select_fields()`. multiple 50 `doi` openalex `id`s provided, request automatically split chunks 50 named list URLs returned.

title: Build an OpenAlex request (httr2) — pro_query

what: Sample rows from Parquet files using DuckDB reservoir sampling — sample_parquet_n
title: Sample rows from Parquet files using DuckDB reservoir sampling — sample_parquet_n
text: Draw uniform random sample n rows one Parquet files using DuckDB's SQL USING SAMPLE reservoir(n ROWS) clause. sampling performed entirely inside DuckDB, full dataset never loaded R. well-suited large Parquet corpora (e.g. OpenAlex works) want random subset rows without materialising whole table.

title: Sample rows from Parquet files using DuckDB reservoir sampling — sample_parquet_n
code: sample_parquet_n(path, n, seed = NULL, con = NULL, select = NULL)

title: Sample rows from Parquet files using DuckDB reservoir sampling — sample_parquet_n
text: path Character scalar. Path glob pointing one Parquet files, understood DuckDB's parquet_scan() table function. example, "spc_corpus/output/chapter_3/corpus/*.parquet". n Integer scalar. Number rows sample. n larger total number rows dataset, DuckDB returns rows. seed Optional integer scalar. supplied, REPEATABLE(seed) clause added DuckDB query repeated calls input data seed return sample. NULL (default), sample forced reproducible DuckDB level. con Optional DBIConnection existing DuckDB database. NULL (default), function creates temporary -memory DuckDB instance, uses query, shuts returning. connection supplied, left open modified beyond running sampling query. select Optional character vector column names return. NULL (default), columns returned (equivalent SELECT *). Column names passed DBI::dbQuoteIdentifier() safely handle special characters. requested column exist Parquet schema, DuckDB raise error.

title: Sample rows from Parquet files using DuckDB reservoir sampling — sample_parquet_n
text: data.frame n rows, containing uniform random sample union Parquet files matched path, restricted columns specified select (columns select NULL).

title: Sample rows from Parquet files using DuckDB reservoir sampling — sample_parquet_n
text: function delegates following SQL pattern (simplified): Using reservoir(n ROWS) gives exact uniform sample size n rows dataset (unless n exceeds total row count, case rows returned). Note path argument passed directly DuckDB's parquet_scan() function, can use: single Parquet file: "works.parquet" glob many files: "works/*.parquet" directory, depending DuckDB version/configuration. con NULL, function creates -memory DuckDB database. want reuse DuckDB instance multiple queries (performance reasons control pragmas), can create DuckDB connection pass via con.
code: SELECT [columns] FROM parquet_scan('path/to/files/*.parquet') USING SAMPLE reservoir(n ROWS) [REPEATABLE (seed)]

title: Sample rows from Parquet files using DuckDB reservoir sampling — sample_parquet_n
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Sample 1,000 rows from a directory of Parquet files sample_df <- sample_parquet_duckdb(   path = "spc_corpus/output/chapter_3/corpus/*.parquet",   n = 10000L,   seed = 1234 )  # Sample only a subset of columns sample_df_small <- sample_parquet_duckdb(   path = "spc_corpus/output/chapter_3/corpus/*.parquet",   n = 10000L,   seed = 1234,   select = c("id", "doi", "citation", "author_abbr", "display_name", "ab") )  # Reuse a DuckDB connection for multiple samples con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb()) on.exit(DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE), add = TRUE)  s1 <- sample_parquet_duckdb(   path = "openalex_works/*.parquet",   n = 500L,   seed = 42,   con = con )  s2 <- sample_parquet_duckdb(   path = "openalex_works/*.parquet",   n = 500L,   seed = 777,   con = con ) } # }

title: openalexPro 0.4.1
text: Standardised progressbar handling Changed default pages 1,000 10,000 Refactored pro_query removed multiple_ids argument using Claude expanded tests added vignette. Added creation 00_completed output directory json, jsonl parquet folders upon successful completion Changed api key email handling. Removed oap_mail()_ oap_apikey() simplified handling api key email use environmental variables openalexPro.email openalexPro.apikey Added unified schema inference pro_request_jsonl_parquet() prevent schema conflicts reading combined Parquet datasets. New sample_size parameter controls schema inference sampling. fixes “Unsupported cast string struct” errors fields different types across JSONL files (e.g., apc_paid null files struct others). Removed harmonize_parquet_schemata() longer needed new unified schema inference. Increased default n umber pages read request_json() 1000 10000 allow initially planned 2,000,000 work download.

title: openalexPro 0.4.0
text: CI coverage tweaks CRAN readiness. splitting snowball functionality openalexSnowball

title: openalexPro 0.3.1
text: Added pro_fetch() project_folder support structured outputs. Added progress reporting parallelization pro_request_jsonl(). Added sample_parquet_n() random sampling utilities select support. Improved count_only output return data frame error column.

title: openalexPro 0.3.0
text: Added count_only support pro_request() related helpers. Added DOI handling improvements API call fixes.

title: openalexPro 0.2.0
text: Introduced pro_query() package-native query builder chunking. Added snowball search utilities citation edge extraction workflow. Expanded conversion pipeline tests VCR-based API fixtures. Added extract_doi() helpers compatibility reporting artifacts.
